channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: S'dumo Mtshali Shares Real-Life Impact of Playing Despised Character Jonasi in 'The Polygamist'
item.description: S'dumo Mtshali, the South African actor playing villain Janasi Gomora in Netflix drama *The Polygamist*, has opened up about the challenges of portraying a deeply flawed character. During a CNN interv...

item.title: Father of crypto casino mogul's mansion destroyed in suspicious fire
item.description: A $16 million mansion in Coffs Harbour, owned by the father of crypto casino billionaire Ed Craven, has been destroyed in a fire deemed 'suspicious' by police. The incident, which occurred on July 22,...

item.title: Madhya Pradesh passes Uniform Civil Code bill to ban polygamy and regulate live-in relationships
item.description: The Madhya Pradesh Assembly has passed the Uniform Civil Code Bill, aiming to ban polygamy and introduce strict regulations for live-in relationships. The bill, introduced by the BJP-led government, s...

item.title: High Court to Rule on President Ramaphosa's Impeachment Case
item.description: The Western Cape High Court is set to deliver its judgment on Friday regarding President Cyril Ramaphosa's urgent application to interdict the Section 89 committee investigating the Phala Phala scanda...

item.title: Remco Evenepoel Secures Back-to-Back Tour de France Stage Wins Amid Teammate's Injury
item.description: In a dramatic turn of events during the Tour de France, Remco Evenepoel claimed victory in stage 16 with a brilliant time trial performance, marking his second consecutive stage win. The race took a b...

item.title: Latest Innovations in Transportation Technology and Military Applications
item.description: The article highlights recent advancements in transportation technology, focusing on both civilian and military applications. Key developments include the Z4 blended wing jet, which promises a 5,000 n...

item.title: Sandulela Asanda's 'Black Burns Fast' Redefines Queer Cinema in South Africa
item.description: Filmmaker Sandulela Asanda’s debut feature, Black Burns Fast, is reshaping the landscape of queer cinema in South Africa by offering a nuanced portrayal of black queer identity. The coming-of-age film...

item.title: Perth CBD Redevelopment Approved: $240M Project Includes Hotel, Retail, and Heritage Buildings
item.description: A $240 million redevelopment project for Perth's CBD has received final approval, marking a significant transformation of the St Martins Centre. The plan includes converting three office towers into a...

item.title: Police Recover Seven Stolen Motorcycles in Anti-Trafficking Operation
item.description: The police have successfully recovered seven stolen motorcycles as part of an ongoing anti-trafficking operation. This achievement comes amid efforts to combat vehicle theft and related crimes across ...

item.title: Viral TikTok Clip Shows Late Elvis Otieno's Phone Still Receiving Calls
item.description: A TikToker shared a video of Elvis Otieno, known as 'Beast', whose phone continued to receive calls after his death. The clip, posted on July 21, 2026, shows the locked screen with incoming calls from...

item.title: Senate impeachment court dismisses senator-judges' reservations on Sara Duterte records
item.description: The Senate impeachment court has clarified that the reservations raised by some senator-judges regarding the issuance of subpoenas for Vice President Sara Duterte's and her husband's records will not ...

item.title: Pakistan Condemns Houthi Threats to Saudi Shipping in Red Sea
item.description: Pakistan's Foreign Office has strongly condemned the threats issued by the Houthis against Saudi Arabia and commercial shipping in the Red Sea, emphasizing that such actions violate international law ...

item.title: Evacuation Orders Issued for Boston Bar Residents Amid Rapidly Spreading Wildfire
item.description: The Fraser Valley Regional District has issued an urgent evacuation order for remaining residents of Boston Bar, British Columbia, due to a new wildfire that has rapidly expanded south of the communit...

item.title: Impeachment trial proceeds as Sara Duterte's international travel continues
item.description: The House prosecution panel confirmed that Vice President Sara Duterte's foreign trips will not interfere with the ongoing impeachment trial against her. House prosecutor Zia Adiong stated that while ...

item.title: US Continues Nightly Attacks on Iran Amid Escalating Regional Tensions
item.description: The US has intensified its military operations against Iran, marking the 11th consecutive night of strikes. These attacks, coordinated by CENTCOM, target Iranian military assets like drone storage fac...

item.title: Chelsea's £117m Morgan Rogers signing: Integration under Xabi Alonso and tactical implications
item.description: Chelsea's £117m acquisition of Morgan Rogers from Aston Villa has sparked significant interest due to his potential to enhance the squad under Xabi Alonso. The transfer, the fourth most expensive in f...

item.title: Nadra Officials Arrested for Involvement in Fake Documents for Afghan Nationals
item.description: The Federal Investigation Agency (FIA) has arrested four individuals, including National Database and Registration Authority (Nadra) officials, in Karachi for allegedly facilitating the production of ...

item.title: 160 people rescued in anti-trafficking operation
item.description: A major anti-trafficking operation conducted by Tanzanian authorities has successfully rescued over 160 individuals from human trafficking networks. The operation, which took place on July 22, 2026, i...

item.title: Mathematicians rethink AI after AI-assisted discovery of Jacobian conjecture counterexample
item.description: A mathematician at Anthropic used its large language model Fable 5 to find a counterexample to the Jacobian conjecture, a longstanding problem in algebraic geometry. This simple yet groundbreaking res...

item.title: Montreal politician supports interprovincial alcohol sales deal but calls for legal reforms
item.description: The article discusses Quebec politician Pierre Fréchette's support for a proposed agreement allowing alcohol sales across provincial borders, while emphasizing the need for legislative changes to impl...
